close_reason: CachingStore<S: SecretStore> exposes the bound because it IS the constraint — the type wraps a SecretStore. This is correct type design, not leakage.
description: CachingStore<S: SecretStore> in secretx-cache/src/lib.rs uses the SecretStore bound directly in the public generic parameter. This means the bound is part of the public API contract: if SecretStore ever gains a new required method (which is a breaking change to SecretStore itself, yes) callers would need to update impls, and more practically, callers cannot use CachingStore with a concrete type unless that type implements SecretStore. Currently this is the intended design, but it means the caching layer cannot be used with any future richer trait (e.g. a hypothetical StreamingSecretStore) without a new generic wrapper. More concretely: the thundering-herd limitation (concurrent get() calls all hit the backend before the first returns) is documented at the module level but not in CachingStore::new(). Callers reading only rustdoc for new() get no warning. Fix: add the thundering-herd caveat directly to the CachingStore::new() doc comment so it appears in the most likely-to-be-read location.
title: CachingStore<S> exposes SecretStore bound in public type
